obsolete_functionality.reason: Leftover NamedTemporaryFile scaffold in test_exact_match_returns_true creates an unused /tmp file and dead variables: the NamedTemporaryFile context manager produces a file and the variables `sid` and `proper_path` derived from it are never referenced. The test immediately falls through to use the hardcoded `test_sid` and `grant_path` instead. The NamedTemporaryFile block is also not cleaned up in the try/finally.
obsolete_functionality.recommendation: Remove the NamedTemporaryFile block entirely (lines 121-127). The test only needs `test_sid = 'test-read-grant-unit'` and `grant_path = f'/tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-{test_sid}.json'`.

obsolete_functionality.reason: Stale inline comment in consume_grant_for_posttool reads '# Mirrors posttool-allowlist-consume.py:34 — || before | (double-newline for ||)'. After the consolidation refactor, this logic now lives inside allowlist.py itself rather than in posttool-allowlist-consume.py; the cross-file reference is no longer accurate.
obsolete_functionality.recommendation: Replace the comment with: '# Mirrors pre-refactor _split_bash_command semantics: replace || before |.'

advisory_findings.reason: OBSERVATION_ONLY: _is_overnight_active() is explicitly documented as 'retained as dead code for reference' since the 2026-04-25 always-on change. The docstring module comment at line 72 records why it was retained (to document the rationale for removing the overnight-context gate). Deleting it would weaken the inline safety rationale.
advisory_findings.recommendation: Do not delete. Keep as documented historical reference unless the safety rationale is moved to an external document.

advisory_findings.reason: OBSERVATION_ONLY: Uses hardcoded python3 throughout (lines 10, 18, 22, 35, 50) while pretool-bash-safety.sh uses the $PYTHON_BIN fallback pattern (CLAUDE_PYTHON_BIN env -> venv/bin/python -> python). This pre-existing inconsistency was not introduced by this diff; only the inline grant-check block was updated (to delegate to lib/allowlist.read_grant) but the surrounding python3 calls remain. If addressed, all invocations should be migrated consistently to a PYTHON_BIN fallback; the guard logic must not be removed.
advisory_findings.recommendation: Follow-up task: add PYTHON_BIN resolution header to pretool-write-guard.sh consistent with pretool-bash-safety.sh lines 8-13.

orphaned_subagents.note: Full-repo scan result — advisory only per default-safe rule (no changed-files scope, not blocking)

unreferenced_scripts.note: Full-repo scan result — advisory only, not blocking

summary.notes: All findings introduced-in-diff are minor. Advisory findings are pre-existing and non-blocking per default-safe rule. The repo-wide mandatory script findings are full-scan results with introduced_in_diff=null and are advisory/non-blocking per spec AC-2.6(b) default-safe rule.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed findings 1 and 2 are valid (same root cause, should be merged into one finding). Finding 3 reframed as a stale comment issue rather than a behavioral split difference. Finding 4 reclassified as OBSERVATION_ONLY — the dead function is intentionally retained for safety documentation. Finding 5 confirmed as OBSERVATION_ONLY pre-existing issue.

findings.rationale: Merged into single finding with combined description covering both the dead variable and the leaked temp file.

findings.issue: Finding 3 mis-characterized as || split behavioral difference; real issue is the stale cross-file comment reference
findings.proposed_fix: Rewrite as stale comment finding: 'Mirrors posttool-allowlist-consume.py:34' reference is stale after consolidation.
findings.rationale: Finding 3 reworded to focus on the stale comment rather than the functionally-equivalent || split.

findings.issue: Finding 4 incorrectly framed as a deletion candidate; _is_overnight_active is intentionally retained for safety documentation rationale
findings.proposed_fix: Mark as OBSERVATION_ONLY — do not delete unless safety rationale is preserved elsewhere.
